name: modal-morph
title: Modal Morph
description: A small card expands into a full panel, shared-element style: both layouts measured once at mount, the container morphs by transform only (never width/height tweens) while keyed children counter-scale and re-flow onto their own measured targets as pure functions of time.
evidence: Wave M9 experiment: the FLIP shared-element grow (Codrops recipes, wild-scout)

variables.label: From scale
variables.description: Multiplies the authored card footprint, clamped 0.5 to 1.5.

variables.label: Expand at
variables.description: Seconds after mount when the card grows into the panel.

variables.label: Register
variables.description: Morph feel. Snappy is faster with a settle capped at 1.02 of the target rect.

variables.label: Accent
variables.description: Accent color: green rides --brand, blue rides --accent, violet rides --accent-2.

variables.label: Exit
variables.description: Optional departure. Default none: the settled panel holds until the frame cuts.
